keywords: San Antonio Spurs @ New York Knicks
article.linkText: Knicks win NBA Cup after rallying to beat Spurs 124-113
article.story: LAS VEGAS -- — It's been more than half a century since the Knicks hung an NBA championship banner inside Madison Square Garden. The wait since 1973 to add another one didn't end on Tuesday night, but New York can make room for the NBA Cup banner. OG Anunoby scored 28 points, Jalen Brunson had 25, and the Knicks rallied to beat Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs 124-113. “This is great and we’re going to enjoy this,” Brunson said. “But once we leave tomorrow, we’re moving on.” This is a championship roster — NBA Cup MVP Brunson, Josh Hart and Mikal Bridges cut down the nets in college at Villanova — with high hopes of representing the Eastern Conference in the NBA Finals. “This is a single-elimination tournament when you get to a certain point, so every game counts,” New York coach Mike Brown said. “There was pressure on every game if you expect to be who you think you are. When you're able to have success, it breeds confidence in everybody in the organization.” New York's Karl-Anthony Towns had 16 points and 11 rebounds despite battling a calf injury. He went into the locker room with a minute left before halftime and later exited for the bench with 5:06 remaining in the third quarter and didn't return until late in the fourth. Dylan Harper led the Spurs with 21 points, Wembanyama scored 18 and De'Aaron Fox had 16. Wembanyama excused himself early from the postgame news conference because he said “just lost somebody” on Tuesday. New York dominated inside, outrebounding the Spurs 59-42, with Mitchell Robinson collecting 15 boards, including 10 on the offensive end in 18 minutes. That helped give the Knicks a 56-44 edge in points in the lane. The Spurs, however, led for much of the game before the Knicks went on a 13-1 run that began late in the third quarter to go up 100-95. New York never trailed again. “After 25 games in, I believe that we’ve shown some signs that we can be a pretty good team," Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said. “We’ve also shown that we have a lot of areas of improvement. I think that’s where we were living today.” The Knicks got more than a trophy. Each player with a standard contract got an extra $318,560 for winning, making the total $530,933 for even reaching the final. This game doesn't count in the standings, so both teams remain 18-7, putting them atop their respective divisions. Just making the final bodes well for both teams. The previous four finalists — Los Angeles Lakers, Indiana, Oklahoma City and Milwaukee — also made the playoffs. The Pacers made the Eastern Conference finals in 2024, and the Thunder won the NBA championship last season. Up next Knicks: At Indiana on Thursday night. Spurs: Host Washington on Thursday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/NBA

video.caption: The Knicks pull away in the fourth quarter to defeat the Spurs and capture the third NBA Cup title.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: Anthony Edwards [1296x729]

images.caption: Cade Cunningham and the Pistons have raced out to a 2-0 series advantage over the Cavs.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are big underdogs against the defending champion Thunder, and Game 1 only lengthened their odds.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.caption: The Knicks have surged to +850 to win the NBA Finals following the first-round exit by the Boston Celtics.

images.caption: The 76ers' odds to win the NBA Finals have marginally shortened after beating the Celtics in seven games.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson #11 of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Atlanta Hawks during Round One Game Six of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on April 30, 2026 at State Farm Arena in Atlanta, Georgia.
images.caption: After entering the playoffs 18-1 to win the NBA championship, the Knicks enter the conference semifinals at 19-1.

images.caption: LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers were 150-1 long shots to win the title entering their first-round series against the Houston Rockets.

images.name: OKC Thunder [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder enter the playoffs as the favorites to win it all.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.caption: Jayson Tatum averaged 21.8 PPG in 16 regular-season games leading up to the playoffs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images

images.caption: The Lakers miss the perimeter shooting that Austin Reaves provides.
images.name: Austin Reaves [1296x729]

images.alt: Luka Doncic #77 of the Los Angeles Lakers handles the ball during the game against the Miami Heat on March 19, 2026 at Kaseya Center in Miami, Florida.
images.caption: The Lakers' championship odds are on the move again after Luka Doncic's injury.
images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.alt: Los Angeles Lakers forward LeBron James (23) scores on a breakaway dunk during the first half of an NBA basketball game against the Orlando Magic, Saturday, March 21, 2026, in Orlando, Fla.
images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are on a roll as March winds down.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 and Devin Vassell #24 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the New Orleans Pelicans on January 25, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Cade Cunningham #2 of the Detroit Pistons handles the ball during the game against the Denver Nuggets on February 3, 2026 at Little Caesars Arena in Detroit, Michigan.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
images.name: Jamal Murray  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers on December 29, 2025 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs have seen their odds to win the NBA championship shorten in the past week.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

videos.description: The Knicks pull away in the fourth quarter to defeat the Spurs and capture the third NBA Cup title.
videos.headline: Knicks surge late to win NBA Cup championship

tracking.trackingName: COM_NBA Highlight (Knicks surge late to win NBA Cup championship) 2025/12/16 ESHEET SOTFULL

videos.description: Karl-Anthony Towns drops a sharp pass inside to OG Anunoby for the Slam.
videos.headline: KAT feeds OG for a 'spicy dish'

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (OG Anunoby skies for big slam) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: Stephon Castle finds Devin Vassell soaring to the rim for a powerful poster finish.
videos.headline: Castle and Vassell connect on a 'rack attack'

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Devin Vassell elevates for jam.) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: Dylan Harper is automatic from 3-point land
videos.headline: Dylan Harper buries triple vs. New York Knicks

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Dylan Harper buries triple vs. New York Knicks) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: Victor Wembanyama rises above the defense and slams home the reverse alley-oop.
videos.headline: Wemby throws down the reverse lob

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Wemby throws down the reverse lob) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: Victor Wembanyama drills a big 3 and follows it up with a putback finish.
videos.headline: Wemby comes alive in the 3rd with impressive buckets

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Wemby comes alive in the third with buckets) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: Jordan Clarkson fired up after 3-pointer puts Knicks ahead
videos.headline: Clarkson puts NY in front with a 3

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Jordan Clarkson knocks down the trey) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: OG Anunoby buries a wide-open corner 3-pointer to put the game away.
videos.headline: Anunoby hits the clutch 3 from the corner

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Anunoby hits the dagger from the corner) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: OG Anunoby delivers a strong performance with 28 points to help secure an NBA Cup victory over the Spurs.
videos.headline: OG Anunoby shines with 28 as Knicks win NBA Cup

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (OG Anunoby shines with 28 as Knicks win NBA Cup) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: The New York Knicks lifts its first-ever NBA Cup trophy following an entertaining win over the San Antonio Spurs.
videos.headline: New York raises NBA Cup for first time

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (New York raises Emirates NBA Cup) 2025/12/17 ESHEET

videos.description: Jalen Brunson joins "SportsCenter" after his New York Knicks beat the San Antonio Spurs to win the NBA Cup.
videos.headline: Brunson: Winning NBA Cup a 'steppingstone' for Knicks

tracking.trackingName: COM_NBA Interview (Brunson: Winning NBA Cup a 'steppingstone' for Knicks) 2025/12/17 ESHEET
